results.title: Uruguay becomes first country to ratify EU-Mercosur agreement
results.snippet: há 4 dias — Uruguay's House of Representatives approved the EU-Mercosur agreement, formally ratifying it, the first South American country to do so.

results.title: Uruguay Economy, Politics and GDP Growth Summary - The Economist Intelligence Unit
results.snippet: Downside risks to our forecast include a sharper than expected economic contraction in Argentina and slower than anticipated growth in Brazil. Relations with Argentina are improving, as that country's right-wing government is easing import restrictions and is likely to support Mr Lacalle's push to create a more market-friendly Mercosur (a customs union that includes Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay).

results.title: Uruguay | Economic Indicators | Moody's Analytics
results.snippet: Uruguay has a free market economy characterized by an export-oriented agricultural sector, a well-educated workforce, and high levels of social spending. results.title: EU to provisionally implement Mercosur deal - Valor International
results.snippet: há 3 dias — The European Union will provisionally implement its trade agreement with Mercosur following ratification by Argentina and Uruguay on Thursday.

results.title: Uruguay Overview: Development news, research, data | World Bank
results.snippet: The WBG’s 2023–2027 Country Partnership Framework (CPF) with Uruguay defines a forward-looking agenda focused on three strategic priorities: Adapting the economy to climate change and strengthening fiscal resilience.

results.title: Economic and political overview in Uruguay
results.snippet: According to preliminary government figures, Uruguay's goods exports reached USD 12.84 billion in 2024. The top exports included cellulose, beef, soybeans, dairy products, and beverage concentrate.
